meaning: adj.对...没有感觉的,感觉迟钝的
examples: We become insensitive to our experience, which means we process less information, and time seems to speed up.
There is nothing sinful or immoral about being emotional, unless your emotions make you inhuman, insensitive and cruel to others.
He had become insensitive to cold.
The insensitive handling of the news prompted its fair share of Internet jabs at Karp.
Labour MP Dame Anne Begg said that Atos's sponsorship of the athletes' equipment was insensitive.
